title: Part 3 – Entraînement 1 : conversations RH & entreprise
instructions: Écoutez chaque conversation, puis répondez aux trois questions.

items.audioText: Woman: I heard there's an opening in the product development team. Do you know if it's been posted internally yet? Man: Yes, it went up on the intranet this morning. The deadline to apply is next Friday. Woman: Great, I've been wanting to move into that department for a while. Do I need my manager's approval to apply? Man: You don't need approval to apply, but if you get an interview, you're expected to inform your current manager.

questions.prompt: What is the woman interested in?
choices: A promotion in her current team
An opening in the product development team
questions.explanation: "I heard there's an opening in the product development team" — elle veut postuler à ce poste.

questions.prompt: When is the application deadline?
questions.explanation: "The deadline to apply is next Friday".

questions.prompt: What must the woman do if she gets an interview?
choices: Submit a written request to HR
questions.explanation: "if you get an interview, you're expected to inform your current manager".

items.audioText: Man: The new flexible working hours policy starts next month. Have you read the details? Woman: I skimmed through it. Basically we can start anywhere between seven and ten, right? Man: Exactly. And finish between four and seven accordingly. The only requirement is being available for core hours, which are ten to four. Woman: That works perfectly for me. I'll start at seven to avoid the rush hour.

questions.prompt: What are the speakers discussing?
questions.explanation: "The new flexible working hours policy starts next month".

questions.prompt: What are the mandatory core hours?
questions.explanation: "The only requirement is being available for core hours, which are ten to four".

questions.prompt: What does the woman plan to do?
choices: Start at ten to finish early
Ask for an exception to the policy
Start at seven to avoid rush hour
Work from home on some days
questions.explanation: "I'll start at seven to avoid the rush hour".

items.audioText: Woman: I wanted to talk to you about James. He's been late three times this week and his reports have been incomplete. Man: I noticed that too. Has anything changed in his personal situation recently? Woman: I'm not sure. Maybe we should have a one-on-one to check in before taking any formal action. Man: Agreed. Let's keep it informal for now and give him the chance to explain. I'll set up a meeting with him tomorrow morning.

questions.prompt: What problem are the speakers discussing?
choices: James's repeated lateness and incomplete work
A conflict between James and a colleague
questions.explanation: "He's been late three times this week and his reports have been incomplete".

questions.prompt: What does the woman suggest doing first?
choices: Having an informal one-on-one with James
questions.explanation: "Maybe we should have a one-on-one to check in before taking any formal action".

questions.prompt: What will the man do next?
choices: Set up a meeting with James tomorrow
Assign James to a different project
questions.explanation: "I'll set up a meeting with him tomorrow morning".

items.audioText: Man: We need to hire two more data analysts before the end of the quarter. Have you contacted the recruitment agency? Woman: Yes, I reached out to them yesterday. They said they can have a shortlist ready within ten days. Man: That's a bit slow. Can we also post the roles on our careers page in parallel? Woman: Already done. I published both listings this morning with a three-week application window.

questions.prompt: What does the company need to do?
choices: Hire two data analysts before the end of the quarter
Partner with a new recruitment agency
questions.explanation: "We need to hire two more data analysts before the end of the quarter".

questions.prompt: How long will it take the agency to prepare a shortlist?
questions.explanation: "They said they can have a shortlist ready within ten days".

questions.prompt: What did the woman already do this morning?
choices: Called the agency to speed up the process
Published two job listings on the careers page
Sent the job descriptions by email
questions.explanation: "I published both listings this morning with a three-week application window".

items.audioText: Woman: The orientation session for the new hires is tomorrow at nine. Is everything ready? Man: Almost. I've printed the welcome packs and booked the training room, but I still need to set up the laptops. Woman: How many laptops are you missing? Man: Three of them haven't been configured yet. IT said they'd have them ready by five this evening. Woman: Good. Make sure they're in the room before nine tomorrow.

questions.prompt: When is the orientation session?
questions.explanation: "The orientation session for the new hires is tomorrow at nine".

questions.prompt: What still needs to be done?
choices: Send the agenda to the new hires
questions.explanation: "Three of them haven't been configured yet".

questions.prompt: When will IT have the laptops ready?
choices: By the end of the week
questions.explanation: "IT said they'd have them ready by five this evening".

items.audioText: Man: I'm finalising the agenda for the all-hands meeting next week. Should we include a Q&A session? Woman: Definitely. Employees always appreciate having a chance to ask questions directly. How long are you planning for the whole meeting? Man: About ninety minutes. I was thinking sixty minutes for presentations and thirty for Q&A. Woman: That sounds right. Just make sure someone is moderating the Q&A so it stays on track.

questions.prompt: What is the man working on?
choices: The agenda for an all-hands meeting
questions.explanation: "I'm finalising the agenda for the all-hands meeting next week".

questions.prompt: How long is the Q&A session planned to last?
questions.explanation: "thirty for Q&A".

questions.prompt: What does the woman recommend for the Q&A?
choices: Limiting it to written questions only
questions.explanation: "Just make sure someone is moderating the Q&A so it stays on track".

items.audioText: Woman: I've just come out of the salary review meeting. I think we should adjust the pay scales for the junior analysts — they're below market rate. Man: By how much, roughly? Woman: Around eight to ten percent on average. If we don't act, we risk losing people to competitors. Man: I agree. Let's draft a proposal and present it to the CFO by the end of the month. We'll need payroll data to support it.

questions.prompt: What does the woman want to adjust?
choices: The pay scales for junior analysts
The overtime rates for all staff
questions.explanation: "we should adjust the pay scales for the junior analysts".

questions.prompt: By how much are salaries below market rate?
questions.explanation: "Around eight to ten percent on average".

questions.prompt: What do they plan to do next?
choices: Ask HR to freeze all salaries
Request approval from all department heads
Draft a proposal to present to the CFO
Conduct exit interviews with junior analysts
questions.explanation: "Let's draft a proposal and present it to the CFO by the end of the month".

items.audioText: Man: Are you coming to the team-building event on Saturday? Woman: I wasn't planning to — is it mandatory? Man: It's not mandatory, but the manager mentioned it would be good for team morale. It's at the Go-Kart track near the city centre. Woman: A Go-Kart track? That actually sounds fun. I'll try to make it. What time does it start? Man: Ten in the morning. There'll be lunch provided afterwards.

questions.prompt: What kind of event is being discussed?
questions.explanation: "Are you coming to the team-building event on Saturday?"

questions.prompt: Where will the event take place?
choices: At a park near the office
At a Go-Kart track near the city centre
questions.explanation: "It's at the Go-Kart track near the city centre".

questions.prompt: What will happen after the main activity?
choices: A visit to a partner company
questions.explanation: "There'll be lunch provided afterwards".

items.audioText: Woman: The exit interview for Mrs. Fontaine is scheduled for this afternoon. Have you prepared the questions? Man: Yes, I've got the standard form. Do you want to sit in on it? Woman: No, I think she'll be more candid if it's just you. Make sure you ask about her reasons for leaving and whether there's anything the company could have done differently. Man: Of course. I'll write up a summary report and send it to you by tomorrow.

questions.prompt: What kind of interview is scheduled?
questions.explanation: "The exit interview for Mrs. Fontaine is scheduled for this afternoon".

questions.prompt: Why does the woman not want to attend?
choices: She has another meeting at the same time
She thinks Mrs. Fontaine will be more candid without her
She already spoke with Mrs. Fontaine last week
questions.explanation: "I think she'll be more candid if it's just you".

questions.prompt: What will the man do after the interview?
choices: File the standard form in the archive
Write a summary report and send it by tomorrow
Schedule a follow-up call with Mrs. Fontaine
questions.explanation: "I'll write up a summary report and send it to you by tomorrow".

items.audioText: Man: The company is rolling out a new HR software platform next quarter. Have you been trained on it yet? Woman: I attended the introductory webinar last week. It looks quite intuitive, but the payroll module is complex. Man: I heard they're offering on-site training sessions next month. I'd recommend signing up for those. Woman: Good idea. Do you know if they cover the payroll module specifically? Man: I believe there's a dedicated half-day session just for payroll.

questions.prompt: What is being rolled out next quarter?
questions.explanation: "The company is rolling out a new HR software platform next quarter".

questions.prompt: Which part of the software does the woman find complex?
questions.explanation: "the payroll module is complex".

questions.prompt: What does the man say about the payroll training?
choices: There is a dedicated half-day session for it
It will be covered in a one-hour webinar
questions.explanation: "there's a dedicated half-day session just for payroll".
